all.en: Every summer, I visit my grandmother in the countryside to learn one of her family recipes.
all.zh: 每年夏天，我都會回鄉下探望奶奶，學習一道家傳食譜。

all.en: This year, she promised to teach me her famous beef noodle soup, which takes a whole day.
all.zh: 今年，她答應教我她那道有名的牛肉麵，這道菜需要一整天才能完成。

all.en: She insisted that I write everything in a small notebook, just like she did fifty years ago.
all.zh: 她堅持要我把所有步驟寫在一本小筆記本上，就像她五十年前那樣。

all.en: First, we went to the market early in the morning to choose the freshest beef shank.
all.zh: 首先，我們一大早就去市場，挑選最新鮮的牛腱。

all.en: My grandmother taught me how to identify good meat by its color and firmness.
all.zh: 奶奶教我如何透過顏色與彈性判斷肉的好壞。

all.en: Back home, we slowly browned the beef and added ginger, garlic, and rock sugar.
all.zh: 回到家後，我們慢慢把牛肉煎香，再加入薑、蒜與冰糖。

all.en: The secret ingredient, she whispered, was a small spoon of fermented bean paste.
all.zh: 她悄聲告訴我，秘密的食材是一小匙的豆瓣醬。

all.en: We let the soup simmer for over four hours until the meat became wonderfully tender.
all.zh: 我們讓湯慢火燉煮超過四個小時，直到肉質變得無比軟嫩。

all.en: While we waited, she told me stories about my late grandfather and their wedding day.
all.zh: 等待的同時，她對我訴說已故爺爺與他們婚禮當天的故事。

all.en: When we finally tasted the soup, I felt tears come to my eyes from joy.
all.zh: 當我們終於品嚐到湯時，我因為喜悅而眼眶泛淚。

all.en: This recipe is not just food; it is a precious memory I will pass on to my own children.
all.zh: 這份食譜不只是料理，更是我將傳承給自己孩子的珍貴回憶。

all.en: The next morning, my grandmother showed me how to make her hand-pulled noodles from scratch.
all.zh: 隔天早上，奶奶教我從零開始做她的手拉麵條。

all.en: She kneaded the dough firmly for twenty minutes until it became smooth and elastic.
all.zh: 她把麵團用力揉了二十分鐘，直到變得光滑而有彈性。

all.en: I struggled to stretch the noodles thin enough without breaking them apart.
all.zh: 我努力把麵條拉得夠細，又不讓它斷掉。

all.en: My grandmother laughed kindly and said practice was the only secret to good noodles.
all.zh: 奶奶慈祥地笑著說，練習就是好麵條唯一的秘訣。

all.en: In the afternoon, we picked fresh bok choy and scallions from her small garden.
all.zh: 下午，我們從她的小菜園採了新鮮的青江菜和青蔥。

all.en: She reminded me that simple ingredients often produce the most unforgettable flavors.
all.zh: 她提醒我，簡單的食材往往能做出最令人難忘的味道。

all.en: My cousins arrived just in time to share the steaming bowls around the wooden table.
all.zh: 我的表兄妹們剛好趕來，圍著木桌一起享用熱騰騰的麵碗。

all.en: Everyone praised the soup, but I knew my version was still far from hers.
all.zh: 大家都讚美這碗湯，但我心裡明白自己的版本還遠遠不及她。

all.en: Before I left, she packed a small jar of her homemade bean paste for me to take home.
all.zh: 我離開前，她裝了一小罐自製的豆瓣醬讓我帶回家。

all.en: That winter, I cooked the same soup for my parents and watched their faces light up.
all.zh: 那年冬天，我為父母煮了同樣的湯，看著他們的臉上綻放笑容。

all.en: The following spring, my grandmother sent me a thick envelope with hand-drawn cooking diagrams.
all.zh: 隔年春天，奶奶寄給我一個厚厚的信封，裡面是她手繪的烹飪圖解。

all.en: She had labeled every step in red ink, including small reminders about timing and heat.
all.zh: 她用紅墨水在每個步驟旁加註，連火候和時間的小提醒都寫上了。

all.en: I copied her notes into a digital file so my future children could read them clearly.
all.zh: 我把她的筆記輸入電子檔，這樣我未來的孩子也能看得清清楚楚。

all.en: My mother smiled when she saw the recipe and shared an older version from her wedding day.
all.zh: 媽媽看到食譜時笑了，並分享了她婚禮那天更早的一個版本。

all.en: We compared the two recipes and discovered that the spice ratios had changed over the decades.
all.zh: 我們比對兩份食譜，發現香料的比例在幾十年間已經悄悄改變。

all.en: That summer, I returned to the countryside with my own notebook full of new questions.
all.zh: 那年夏天，我帶著一本寫滿新問題的筆記本，再次回到鄉下。

all.en: Grandmother taught me how to make her plum sauce, which she usually kept secret from guests.
all.zh: 奶奶教我做她那道梅子醬，那是她平時不肯讓客人知道的秘方。

all.en: We spent the afternoon in the garden picking herbs and chatting about her childhood memories.
all.zh: 我們在花園裡度過整個下午，一邊採香草，一邊聊她童年的回憶。

all.en: Before I left, she gave me her old wooden spoon, the one she had used for forty years.
all.zh: 我離開前，她把那支用了四十年的舊木匙交給了我。

all.en: Now every time I cook, I feel her gentle hands guiding mine through every careful step.
all.zh: 現在每當我下廚，總感覺她那雙溫柔的手在引領著我完成每個步驟。

all.en: One quiet evening, I dialed my grandmother to ask about her secret pickled radish recipe.
all.zh: 某個寧靜的傍晚，我打電話給奶奶，請教她的秘製醃蘿蔔食譜。

all.en: She chuckled softly and reminded me that patience was the most important ingredient of all.
all.zh: 她輕聲笑著，提醒我耐心才是最重要的食材。

all.en: The next weekend, I sliced fresh radishes thinly and salted them just as she instructed.
all.zh: 隔週週末，我依照她的指示，把新鮮蘿蔔切薄並仔細抹上鹽。

all.en: While the radishes drained, I prepared a sweet vinegar brine with pink peppercorns and bay leaves.
all.zh: 等蘿蔔出水時，我用粉紅胡椒和月桂葉調好一鍋甜醋醃汁。

all.en: My kitchen filled with a tangy aroma that reminded me strongly of summer afternoons in her village.
all.zh: 廚房裡瀰漫著酸香，讓我強烈想起在她村裡度過的夏日午後。

all.en: I packed the radishes into clean glass jars and labeled each one with her birth year.
all.zh: 我把蘿蔔裝進乾淨的玻璃罐，每罐都寫上她的出生年份。

all.en: Two weeks later, I tasted the pickles and felt a wave of childhood memories rush back.
all.zh: 兩週後，我嚐了這道醃蘿蔔，一股童年回憶頓時湧上心頭。

all.en: I sent one jar to my grandmother by post, wrapped in soft cloth to prevent any cracks.
all.zh: 我用柔軟的布把一罐包好，郵寄給奶奶以避免破裂。

all.en: She called the moment the jar arrived and said the flavor matched her own youthful version exactly.
all.zh: 罐子一到，她立刻打來說味道和她年輕時做的版本一模一樣。

all.en: That phone call reminded me that recipes are bridges connecting generations across both time and distance.
all.zh: 那通電話讓我體會到，食譜是跨越時間與距離、連結世代的橋樑。
